paper_metadata.title: UL2: Unifying Language Learning Paradigms
paper_metadata.abstract: Existing pre-trained models are generally geared towards a particular class of problems. To date, there seems to be still no consensus on what the right architecture and pre-training setup should be. This paper presents a unified framework for pre-training models that are universally effective across datasets and setups. We begin by disentangling architectural archetypes with pre-training objectives -- two concepts that are commonly conflated. Next, we present a generalized & unified perspective for self-supervision in NLP and show how different pre-training objectives can be cast as one another and how interpolating between different objectives can be effective. We then propose Mixture-of-Denoisers (MoD), a pre-training objective that combines diverse pre-training paradigms together. We furthermore introduce a notion of mode switching, wherein downstream fine-tuning is associated with specific pre-training schemes. We conduct extensive ablative experiments to compare multiple pre-training objectives and find that our method pushes the Pareto-frontier by outperforming T5 & GPT-like models across multiple diverse setups. By scaling our model up to 20B parameters, we achieve SOTA performance on 50 well-established supervised finetuning based NLP tasks. Our model also achieve strong results at in-context learning, outperforming 175B GPT-3 on zero-shot SuperGLUE and tripling the performance of T5-XXL on one-shot summarization. On 0-shot MMLU, UL2 20B outperforms T0 and T5 models. UL2 20B also works well with chain-of-thought prompting and reasoning, making it an appealing choice for research into reasoning at a small to medium scale of 20B parameters. Finally, we apply FLAN instruction tuning to the UL2 20B model, achieving MMLU and Big-Bench scores competitive to FLAN-PaLM 62B. We release Flax-based T5X checkpoints for the UL2 20B & Flan-UL2 20B.

extraction_data.innovation: Unifies different language learning paradigms (e.g., autoregressive, masked language modeling) via a Mixture-of-Denoisers (MoD) objective, enabling both fine-tuning and in-context learning capabilities. The MoD objective combines short-span, long-span, and extreme denoising to improve performance across diverse tasks.
extraction_data.extension: Mixture of denoisers enables flexible corruption rates and span lengths, allowing the model to handle both short-span and extreme denoising tasks, improving sample efficiency and generalization.
extraction_data.license: open source (Flax-based T5X model checkpoints released)
extraction_data.research_problem: Unifying diverse language learning paradigms (e.g., autoregressive, masked language modeling) into a single pretraining framework to improve generalization and performance across tasks.
extraction_data.application: Language generation, language understanding, text classification, question answering, commonsense reasoning, long-text reasoning, structured knowledge grounding, information retrieval, in-context learning, chain-of-thought prompting

extraction_data.innovation: Decoder-only variant of UL2 that leverages the same mixture-of-denoisers framework to unify language learning paradigms, enabling efficient text generation and understanding tasks.
extraction_data.application: Text generation, language understanding, few-shot learning
extraction_data.research_problem: Unifying language learning paradigms in a decoder-only architecture for efficient and generalizable language modeling.
extraction_data.extension: GLU layers and T5-style relative position embeddings for improved performance and efficiency in decoder-only architecture.

extraction_data.innovation: Unifies multiple pre-training paradigms (causal LM, prefix LM, span corruption) into a single model using a mixture-of-denoisers objective, enabling better generalization across diverse tasks.
extraction_data.application: Language understanding, text generation, few-shot learning, supervised fine-tuning
extraction_data.research_problem: Unifying diverse language learning paradigms (e.g., autoregressive, bidirectional, and sequence-to-sequence) into a single pre-training framework
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Mixture of denoising objectives (Span Corruption, PrefixLM, Causal LM)
extraction_data.extension: Uses SwiGLU layers and T5-style relative attention to enhance the Transformer backbone.
